methods.name: q1pulse.assembler.instruction_queue.InstructionQueue._wait_register_updates
documentation:
Inserts a NOP when one of the arguments is a register which
has been modified by the previous instruction.
Registers are modified by the arithmetic instructions.

functions.name: q1pulse.instrument.set_exception_on_overload

Setting set_exception_on_overload to False suppresses Exception
when the QRM input is overloaded.

documentation: Base for Q1Pulse exceptions.

classes.name: q1pulse.lang.exceptions.Q1InternalError
documentation:
Raised when something unexpected fails in Q1Pulse
most likely caused by coding error in Q1Pulse.

classes.name: q1pulse.lang.exceptions.Q1NameError
documentation: Raised when a register is not defined, or already defined.

classes.name: q1pulse.lang.exceptions.Q1TypeError

classes.name: q1pulse.lang.exceptions.Q1ValueError

classes.name: q1pulse.lang.exceptions.Q1MemoryError
documentation: Raised when no free register is left to allocate.

classes.name: q1pulse.lang.exceptions.Q1TimingError
documentation: Raised when the timing of commands doesn't fit.

classes.name: q1pulse.lang.exceptions.Q1StateError
documentation: Raised when program or sequence is modified after compilation.

classes.name: q1pulse.lang.exceptions.Q1InputOverloaded
documentation:
Raised when the input of QRM is overloaded during acquisition.
Exception can be suppressed with `q1pulse.set_exception_on_overload(False)`.

classes.name: q1pulse.lang.conditions.ConditionalBlockStatement

methods.name: q1pulse.lang.conditions.ConditionalBlockStatement.close
documentation:
Adds else branch of equal length.
get first RT statement and last RT statement

Statement containing one or more conditions on a set of trigger counters.

The duration of the conditional block is fixed.
Wait times are added at the end of every branch to enforce equal duration.

Some time is needed between the conditional block statement and the
first statement of any else-branch, because it takes time to skip the
statements of the previous branches.
Some time is also needed to skip the statements of subsequent branches.
This could result in some additional execution time for the block.

Branches are added in programming order.

methods.name: q1pulse.sequencer.readout.ReadoutBuilder.acquire_frequency_sweep
documentation:
Aquire `n` values with interval `period` while stepping frequency from `f_start` till `f_stop`.

Args:
    n: number of values to acquire
    period: time between acquisitions
    f_start: start frequency
    f_stop: stop frequency, inclusive
    acq_delay: delay between changing frequency and starting acquisition

Note:
    Experimentally it is determined that the nco_prop_delay should be 146 ns + delay in lines (~ 4 ns/m),
    and acq_delay should be nco_prop_delay + 4 ns.

functions.name: q1pulse.assembler.generator.register_args
documentation:
Signature:
I: integer only; Evaluate expression, allow label.
f: float or int: Evaluate expression, if one float, then all float; label counts as int
F: float only: Evaluate expression, convert to i16
t: time. integer, No register. No conversion.
o: object. No register. No conversion.

class_variables.name: q1pulse.sequencer.sequencer.SequenceBuilder.add_traceback_to_instructions
documentation:
Adding traceback is convenient to find a problem in your script.
However, it makes the compiler pretty slow.
Q1Instrument can suppress the traceback.

methods.name: q1pulse.sequencer.control.ControlBuilder.set_frequency
documentation:
Args:
    frequency (float): nco frequency in Hz

methods.name: q1pulse.sequencer.control.ControlBuilder.shift_phase
documentation:
Args:
    hires_reg:
        Convert register to exactly phase if True, else
        convert register value with relative error of 2e-4.

NOTE:
    When `phase` is a Register many instructions are added for the
    conversion of the `phase` This costs ~188 ns when hires_reg=False.
    When hires_reg=True this costs ~268 ns.

    When `phase` is a constant in q1asm, then the resolution is 1e-9.
    No extra time is needed.

methods.name: q1pulse.sequencer.control.ControlBuilder.set_phase
documentation:
Args:
    hires_reg:
        Convert register to exactly phase if True, else
        convert register value with relative error of 2e-4.

NOTE:
    When `phase` is a Register many instructions are added for the
    conversion of the `phase` This costs ~188 ns when hires_reg=False.
    When hires_reg=True this costs ~268 ns.

methods.name: q1pulse.sequencer.control.ControlBuilder.shaped_pulse
documentation:
When amplitude is None, it is assumed that the gain has already been set to
the right value.
